vocabularyItems.english: to be called / to be named

vocabularyItems.context: the number 1; also used as the indefinite article 'a/an' for common-gender nouns
used in clock expressions: klokken er tre = it is three o'clock
used in clock expressions: halv tre = 2:30 (half towards three)
male friend, or a friend in general
possessive, used with en-words and plurals
possessive, used with en-words and plurals
marital status, not the word for poison
location of a place or building
spatial: on a surface or at a location
spatial: next to or near something
traditional Danish open-faced rye bread with toppings

vocabularyItems.english: to walk / to go on foot
to drive / to go by vehicle

vocabularyItems.context: taking transport, e.g. take the bus
deep happiness or bliss, stronger than glad
as in 'jeg har det godt' — used with 'have det'
as in 'jeg har det dårligt' — used with 'have det'
used with godt/dårligt or an adjective: 'jeg har det godt', 'hun har det svært'
reflexive: 'jeg føler mig glad', 'hun føler sig træt'

vocabularyItems.english: How are you? / How are you feeling?

vocabularyItems.context: asking a shop if it stocks something

vocabularyItems.english: What is that called in Danish?

examples.danish: Jeg hedder Maria og jeg er lærer.
examples.english: My name is Maria and I am a teacher.
He works in Copenhagen.
I come from England.
She is an engineer.
I speak Danish.
You live in Denmark.
What time is it?
When is your birthday?

examples.danish: Det er mandag den tredje oktober.
examples.english: It is Monday the third of October.

examples.danish: Vi mødes den første fredag i måneden.
examples.english: We meet on the first Friday of the month.

examples.danish: en grøn taske / et grønt bord / grønne tasker
examples.english: a green bag / a green table / green bags

examples.danish: en stor bold / et stort hus / store bolde
examples.english: a big ball / a big house / big balls

examples.danish: Det er en lille, blå taske.
examples.english: It is a small, blue bag.
The car is red and big.
That is my brother.

examples.danish: Mit barn hedder Anna, og mine forældre bor i Aarhus.
examples.english: My child is named Anna, and my parents live in Aarhus.
The cat is sitting on the table.
The car is in front of the house.
The park is near the station.
The kitchen is big.
I don't eat meat.
He doesn't drink coffee.
I like fish.

examples.danish: Hun kan ikke lide bitter mad.
examples.english: She doesn't like bitter food.

examples.danish: Gå ligeud og drej til venstre ved kirken.
examples.english: Go straight ahead and turn left at the church.

examples.danish: Tag bussen til stationen og gå derfra.
examples.english: Take the bus to the station and walk from there.
I'm doing well. / I feel fine.

examples.danish: Hun har det ikke godt i dag.
examples.english: She is not feeling well today.
I am tired.
He is nervous about the exam.
It is raining today.
It is very windy in winter.

grammarConcepts.name: Modal verbs (kan, skal, vil, må, bør)

examples.english: May I try this on?
Can I get a receipt?
